_comment: THE HARDWARE -> RECOMMENDATION TABLE. Edit this file freely, then run `lai plan`
to re-evaluate (and `lai models && lai config && lai restart` to apply).
models: registry of runnable GGUF models. fits_vram_gb = VRAM needed fully on GPU;
hybrid_ram_gb = system RAM needed for MoE expert offload (moe models only);
cpu_ok = acceptable speed on CPU alone. verify=true means the repo name should be
checked on Hugging Face before relying on it (newer than this catalog's research).
tiers: first tier whose conditions match the detected hardware wins (top to bottom).
Per-role defaults reference model ids; `lai plan` falls back to the best fitting
alternative automatically if a default does not fit or is missing.

qwen3-coder-30b-a3b.name: Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B-Instruct
qwen3-coder-30b-a3b.why: agent-trained MoE (3B active), 256K native ctx; the intelligence/speed king wherever it fits

qwen3-30b-a3b-thinking.name: Qwen3-30B-A3B-Thinking-2507
qwen3-30b-a3b-thinking.why: planning / architecture / hard debugging companion to the coder

devstral-small.name: Devstral-Small-2507 (24B)
devstral-small.why: dense 24B tuned specifically for coding agents (OpenHands); best dense pick at 16 GB

qwen2.5-coder-14b.name: Qwen2.5-Coder-14B-Instruct
qwen2.5-coder-14b.why: strongest dense coder that fits 12 GB VRAM (June 2026 sources also list a Qwen3-Coder-14B successor - check HF and update this entry); same-family 1.5B draft enables speculative decoding (+30-60%)

qwen2.5-coder-7b.name: Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct
qwen2.5-coder-7b.why: best coder for 8 GB VRAM / 16 GB Macs; 1.5B draft enables speculative decoding when fully on GPU

qwen3-4b.name: Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507
qwen3-4b.why: remarkably capable small generalist for low-spec machines

gemma-4-26b-a4b.name: Gemma 4 26B-A4B (Google)
gemma-4-26b-a4b.why: strongest local GENERALIST of mid-2026 (LiveCodeBench ~80, AIME ~89); 4B-active MoE = same hybrid trick as the Qwen coder. Less agent/tool-call specialized and 128K ctx vs Qwen's 256K - compare on YOUR tasks: lai bench --quality

gemma-4-12b.name: Gemma 4 12B (Google)
gemma-4-12b.why: best dense generalist in the 10-12 GB class (LiveCodeBench ~72); strong alternative to the 14B coder when you want reasoning + code in one model

deepseek-v4-flash.name: DeepSeek V4-Flash (284B-A13B)
deepseek-v4-flash.why: frontier-class open coding (~80 SWE-bench Verified) for multi-GPU rigs (2x 96 GB class); GGUF quants not published yet - `lai refresh` will spot them when they land

qwen3-coder-next.name: Qwen3-Coder-Next (80B-A3B class)
qwen3-coder-next.why: near-frontier agentic coder (~58.7% SWE-bench Verified); hybrid on 24 GB GPU + 64 GB RAM, resident at 48 GB+

qwen3-vl-8b.name: Qwen3-VL-8B-Instruct
qwen3-vl-8b.why: best open VL at this size for screenshots / diagrams / PDFs

qwen3-vl-4b.name: Qwen3-VL-4B-Instruct
qwen3-vl-4b.why: lighter vision option for small GPUs / 16 GB Macs

qwen2.5-coder-3b.name: Qwen2.5-Coder-3B-Instruct
qwen2.5-coder-3b.why: FIM-trained; the autocomplete sweet spot when VRAM allows

qwen2.5-coder-1.5b.name: Qwen2.5-Coder-1.5B-Instruct
qwen2.5-coder-1.5b.why: sub-100ms completions; also the last-resort coder on tiny machines

qwen3-embedding-0.6b.name: Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B
qwen3-embedding-0.6b.why: powers repo semantic search (Qdrant); cheap enough to keep always-on

engines._comment: asset regex patterns for the llama.cpp GitHub release, keyed by <platform>-<vendor-class>

windows-vulkan.note: AMD/Intel GPUs on Windows run best on Vulkan

linux-cuda.note: if no CUDA asset is published, build from source with -DGGML_CUDA=ON

linux-vulkan.note: Vulkan works everywhere; a ROCm source build is ~10-20% faster on supported Radeons

mac-metal.note: Metal is built in; MLX (LM Studio / mlx-lm) is ~10-25% faster but has no llama-swap equivalent - llama.cpp keeps one API everywhere

discovery._comment: Used by `lai refresh`: finds GGUF models newer than this catalog. Tune freely.

cloud._comment: Curated best-value picks shown by `lai cloud list` (verify names/prices on the provider - this is editable data like everything else).

recommended.why: free tier - try cloud at zero cost
near-frontier coding (~80 SWE-V) at ~$0.14/$0.28 per M - the value king
top open SWE-bench Pro, 1M context, multimodal - for huge-repo questions
top quality for the hardest reviews
cheap default for everyday cloud calls

usecases._comment: Overlays applied on top of the hardware tier by `lai plan` (or the UI). Per role: enabled true/false and optional ctx override. Hardware still rules: an enabled role only activates if a model fits.

general.label: General / mixed development
notes: tier defaults, nothing changed

web.label: Web & WordPress (PHP / JS / TS, themes, sites)
notes: vision on: design screenshots -> markup is a daily move
autocomplete gets more context for long template files

mobile.label: Mobile apps (Android / iOS / Flutter / React Native)
extensions: Kotlin / Swift language servers via the platform IDEs
notes: vision prioritized: UI mockups and screenshots are the main input
heavy UI work is best in Android Studio/Xcode with this stack as the API backend

systems.label: Systems & desktop (Go / Rust / C++)
notes: vision off frees VRAM/disk
coder context raised for cross-crate/package reasoning (capped by what the hardware allows)

data-ml.label: Data science & ML (Python, notebooks)
notes: vision on: reading plots and notebook screenshots

game.label: Game development (2D/3D, sprites, gameplay code)
notes: vision on: screenshots of sprites/scenes are daily inputs in game dev

scripts.label: Scripts & DevOps (shell, automation, IaC)
notes: minimal footprint: coder + autocomplete + embeddings only - saves ~25 GB disk vs the full stack

stacks._comment: Project scaffolds for `lai new`. init_cmds use the ecosystem's own generator ({name} = sanitized project name, {python} = current interpreter); files are minimal seeds only where no generator exists. agents.* feed the generated AGENTS.md.

toolchains.hint: winget install GoLang.Go | brew install go | sudo apt install golang
winget install GoLang.Go | brew install go | sudo apt install golang
winget install Kitware.CMake | brew install cmake | sudo apt install cmake build-essential

files.CMakeLists.txt:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.20)
project({name} CXX)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 20)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
add_executable({name} src/main.cpp)

toolchains.hint: install Node.js: winget install OpenJS.NodeJS.LTS | brew install node | https://nodejs.org
winget install OpenJS.NodeJS.LTS | brew install node | https://nodejs.org
optional but recommended for `php -l` lint: winget install PHP.PHP | brew install php | sudo apt install php-cli

files.functions.php:
<?php
add_action('wp_enqueue_scripts', function () {
    wp_enqueue_style('{name}-style', get_stylesheet_uri());
});
add_theme_support('title-tag');
add_theme_support('post-thumbnails');
files.header.php:
<!doctype html>
<html <?php language_attributes(); ?>>
<head>
<meta charset="<?php bloginfo('charset'); ?>">
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
<?php wp_head(); ?>
</head>
<body <?php body_class(); ?>>

agents.run: copy the theme folder into wp-content/themes/ of a local WordPress

toolchains.hint: install Node.js: winget install OpenJS.NodeJS.LTS | brew install node
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8 | brew install dotnet | https://dot.net
winget install Gradle.Gradle | brew install gradle | sdkman: sdk install gradle
install Node.js: winget install OpenJS.NodeJS.LTS | brew install node

static-site.label: Simple website (HTML + CSS, no build tools - great first project)

files.index.html:
Hello from {name}!

Edit me in index.html - then ask your AI to make me amazing.

agents.run: open index.html in a browser (double-click it)

files.main.py:
"""A tiny starter game: a bouncing square. Run: pip install pygame && python main.py"""
import pygame

pygame.init()
screen = pygame.display.set_mode((800, 600))
pygame.display.set_caption("{name}")
clock = pygame.time.Clock()
x, y, dx, dy = 100, 100, 4, 3

running = True
while running:
    for event in pygame.event.get():
        if event.type == pygame.QUIT:
            running = False
    x, y = x + dx, y + dy
    if x < 0 or x > 760:
        dx = -dx
    if y < 0 or y > 560:
        dy = -dy
    screen.fill((13, 16, 23))
    pygame.draw.rect(screen, (77, 163, 255), (x, y, 40, 40))
    pygame.display.flip()
    clock.tick(60)

pygame.quit()

toolchains.hint: optional - download the Godot 4 editor from godotengine.org and open this folder

files.project.godot:
; Engine configuration file - open this folder with the Godot 4 editor.
config_version=5

[application]
config/name="{name}"
run/main_scene="res://main.tscn"
config/features=PackedStringArray("4.2")

agents.run: open the folder in the Godot editor and press F5

tiers.label: Apple Silicon, 48+ GB unified memory
notes: 96/128 GB Macs: try qwen3-coder-next via `lai set coder qwen3-coder-next`

tiers.label: Apple Silicon, 28-48 GB unified memory
notes: the 30B MoE leaves little headroom at 32 GB - close heavy apps while it runs

tiers.label: Apple Silicon, 14-28 GB unified memory
notes: 24 GB Macs can try qwen2.5-coder-14b via `lai set coder qwen2.5-coder-14b`

tiers.label: Apple Silicon, under 14 GB unified memory
notes: 8 GB is below comfortable - expect to close other apps

tiers.label: NVIDIA/AMD GPU, 40+ GB VRAM
notes: serious option: qwen3-coder-next fully resident; also consider vLLM for parallel agents at this scale

tiers.label: NVIDIA/AMD GPU, 20-40 GB VRAM
notes: with 64+ GB system RAM, qwen3-coder-next runs hybrid at ~15-25 tok/s - near-frontier quality
gemma-4-26b-a4b fits fully resident here too - the best generalist alternative; compare with lai bench --quality

tiers.label: NVIDIA/AMD GPU, 14-20 GB VRAM
notes: alternative: qwen3-coder-30b-a3b hybrid is faster but needs 28+ GB system RAM

tiers.label: NVIDIA/AMD GPU, 10-14 GB VRAM
notes: with 28+ GB RAM the MoE hybrid beats the dense 14B: `lai set coder qwen3-coder-30b-a3b`
gemma-4-12b is the strongest generalist at this size: `lai set coder gemma-4-12b`

tiers.label: NVIDIA/AMD GPU 6-10 GB VRAM + 28+ GB RAM
notes: MoE expert-offload tier: 30B-class intelligence at ~8-13 tok/s; agents work but are slow

thinker off by default: swapping two 18.6 GB models through 32 GB RAM evicts each other (30-60 s per swap) - re-enable with `lai set thinker qwen3-30b-a3b-thinking` if you plan in long uninterrupted sessions

tiers.label: NVIDIA/AMD GPU, 6-10 GB VRAM
notes: a RAM upgrade to 32 GB unlocks the much smarter MoE hybrid tier

tiers.label: No usable GPU, 28+ GB RAM
notes: MoE on CPU is usable for chat (~5-8 tok/s) but too slow for agents; autocomplete disabled (latency)

tiers.label: No usable GPU, under 12 GB RAM
notes: below the comfort floor for local coding AI - consider this a demo configuration
